data.title: Tech funding soars 439.74% MoM in April
data.description:
Year-on-year, tech funding rose 546.72%.

Tech funding surged 439.74% month-on-month (MoM) to $434m in April from $80.4m in March, Traxcn reported.

Year-on-year (YoY), tech funding rose 546.72%.+

Late-stage funding accounted for 80.7% of total funding during the period, whilst early-stage funding made up the remaining 18.7%.

Top deals included Supabase ($200), Thunes ($150m), and Cinch ($28.8m).

The top investors were Accel, Z Venture Capital, and Tin Men, each participating in one deal. Meanwhile, Vitruvian Partners led private equity funds, and Y Combinator led the incubators.

data.title: Saab and ST Engineering to co-develop field hospitals in Asia-Pacific
data.description:
The agreement aims to provide scalable and adaptable medical support systems for a range of operations.

Saab Australia and ST Engineering have signed a teaming agreement to jointly develop deployable health solutions for combat field hospitals in the Asia-Pacific region.

The agreement aims to provide scalable and adaptable medical support systems for a range of operations, including military deployments, humanitarian aid, and peacekeeping missions.

It builds on an existing relationship between the two companies and targets shared interests in regional defence and emergency response markets.

According to Saab Australia, the company brings experience in delivering deployable health capabilities to defence forces in Australia and Europe. ST Engineering’s contribution will include expertise in unmanned integrated systems and automation technologies.

Both companies stated that the collaboration is intended to enhance the delivery of mobile medical care in diverse operational environments, with a focus on rapid response and consistent treatment capacity.

data.title: Two freehold properties at Geylang Lorong 4 and 12 up for sale
data.description:
The guide prices are $4.8m for 47 Lorong 4 Geylang and $9.5m for 4 & 6 Lorong 12 Geylang.

Knight Frank Singapore has launched an expression of interest (EOI) exercise for two freehold properties at 47 Lorong 4 Geylang and 4 & 6 Lorong 12 Geylang.

The guide price for 47 Lorong 4 Geylang is $4.8m, whilst 4 & 6 Lorong 12 Geylang has a guide price of $9.5m.

Both sites are zoned “Commercial/Institution” under the URA Master Plan 2019, with a plot ratio of 2.8.

The two properties are near Kallang MRT station (East-West Line) and are within a 15-minute drive to the CBD and Orchard Road.

They are also well-connected by major expressways, including the KPE, PIE, and ECP, as well as arterial routes such as Nicoll Highway, Geylang Road, and Sims Way.

The 47 Lorong 4 Geylang is situated on a land area of 2,046 sq ft. This 3-storey walk-up apartment has an estimated floor area of 4,905 sq ft and a maximum allowable GFA of 5,729 sq ft.

Meanwhile, 4 & 6 Lorong 12 Geylang comprises two adjoining land plots with a combined land area of 3,659 sq ft. This site features a 2-storey building with an attic and offers a maximum GFA of 10,245 sq ft.

The EOI exercise closes on 10 June at 3:00 p.m.

data.title: Landed home prices breach $2,000 PSF in Q1 2025
data.description:
It was driven by strong income growth, wealth build-up and decline in interest rates..

Prices of landed homes breached $2,000 per square foot for the first time, representing a 3.3% increase quarter-on-quarter (QoQ), according to Huttons. This is the fastest growth since Q3 2022.

It was driven by strong income growth, wealth build-up, and decline in interest rates.

Whilst transaction volume decreased 5.3% QoQ to 412 units, it remains the highest first-quarter sales figure since 2021.

Looking ahead, Huttons said the market will be influenced by global tariffs. Demand-wise, locals may delay entering the landed homes market, waiting for more clarity and stability.

The ultra high net-worth individuals may also be reassessing their options due to the disruption. 

Huttons also added that Singapore’s stability could make it an attractive safe haven, with some even considering citizenship.

data.title: SATS taps Sandeep Sakharkar as new chief digital officer
data.description:
His appointment is effective from 17 March.

SATS appointed Sandeep Sakharkar as chief digital officer (CDO) effective 17 March.

Sakharkar brings over two and a half decades of experience in emerging technologies.

He will be responsible for the development and execution of the company’s digital strategy, including the integration of data analytics, automation, and AI into the company’s global operations.

Prior to his appointment, Sakharkar held senior roles at Johnson & Johnson, Footlocker, and GXO Logistics. He is also the founder and non-executive chairman of AI startup Datanoetic.

“As the logistics and aviation industries continue to evolve, digital excellence is critical to staying ahead. With Sandeep’s proven track record, we are poised to unlock improvements in data-driven decision-making, operational efficiency, and customer-centric innovation,” Kerry Mok, president and CEO of SATS said.

data.title: HDB resale prices up 9.3% YoY in April
data.description:
Resale prices inched up 0.7% MoM.

HDB resale prices rose 9.3% year-on-year (YoY) in April, SRX and 99.co reported.

Prices for 3-room flats increased the most at 10.2%, followed by 4-room (9.5%), 5-room (8.5%), and executive flats (6.8%). non-mature Estate prices rose 9.6%, whilst mature estates saw a 9.1% increase.

Month-on-month, resale prices inched up 0.7% from March.

Meanwhile, resale transactions rose 20.8% from March to 2,309 units, but were 3.3% lower compared to April 2024.

Of total transactions, 59.7% were in non-mature estates, whilst 40.3% were in mature estates. By flat type, 3-room flats made up 25.8% of sales, 4-room flats 44.9%, 5-room flats 22.9%, and executive flats 6.3%.

The most expensive resale transaction was a 5-room flat at Lorong 1A Toa Payoh, which sold for $1.49m. The priciest deal in non-mature estates was an executive flat at Hougang Street 21 for $1.25m.

April also saw a spike in the number of million-dollar HDB transactions. A total of 141 flats were sold for at least $1m, up from 108 units in March 2025. These premium sales represented 6.1% of the total resale volume for the month.

Toa Payoh led in million-dollar sales with 27 transactions, followed by Bukit Merah (23), and Queenstown and Kallang/Whampoa with 14 each.

Other high-value sales were recorded across Bishan, Serangoon, Ang Mo Kio, Central Area, Clementi, Hougang, Bedok, Woodlands, Tampines, Punggol, Jurong East, Pasir Ris, Marine Parade, Geylang, Sengkang, Bukit Timah, and Bukit Panjang.

data.title: FLCT posts $0.03 DPU for 1HFY25
data.description:
It also reported an adjusted net property income of $161.3m for the period.

Frasers Logistics & Commercial Asset Management Pte. Ltd., the manager of Frasers Logistics & Commercial Trust (FLCT), has announced a lower distribution per unit (DPU) of $0.03 for the six-month period ended 31 March 2025 (1HFY25).

FLCT reported revenue of $232.3m and adjusted net property income of $161.3m for 1HFY25, representing increases of 7.5% and 1.6% respectively from the same period last year.

The increase was driven by full contributions from Ellesmere Port, completed in December 2023; the acquisition of four German logistics properties in March 2024; the Maastricht Property in the Netherlands, completed in October 2024; and 2 Tuas South Link 1 in Singapore, acquired in November 2024.

These gains were partially offset by higher vacancies at Alexandra Technopark and 357 Collins Street, higher non-recoverable land taxes in Australia, and lower average AUD and EUR exchange rates against the SGD.

Higher interest rates and additional borrowings to fund developments and acquisitions led to increased finance costs.

Distributable income fell to $113m, down from $130.7m a year ago, with 56.9% of management fees paid in cash.

data.title: W Capital Markets defends valuation for Sinarmas Land’s privatisation offer
data.description:
It stands by its estimated share price range of S$0.35 to S$0.361.

W Capital Markets, the independent financial advisor (IFA) for Sinarmas Land, has issued a response to recent media reports and concerns raised by the Securities Investors Association (Singapore) (SIAS) regarding its valuation for the company’s privatisation offer.

The firm defended its use of the Sum-of-the-Parts (SOTP) valuation method, stating that it is a widely accepted and appropriate approach.

W Capital Markets stands by its estimated share price range of $0.35 to $0.361, which reflects the value of Sinarmas Land’s assets, including its Indonesian-listed subsidiaries.

The firm also pointed out that the valuation methods used were carefully considered, taking into account the company’s corporate structure and the independent management of its subsidiaries.

In response to concerns raised about "double discounting" in the valuation of unlisted assets, W Capital Markets clarified that the 20%-22% holding company discount applied is a standard industry practice.

This discount, which reflects the risks and challenges of owning a holding company, is separate from the valuation of Sinarmas Land’s remaining assets, it added.

The firm noted that similar privatisation transactions have typically been completed at a discount to RNAV, supporting the validity of their approach.

W Capital Markets  further addressed claims that its valuation did not account  potential changes after privatisation, stressing that "it is not the IFA’s role to speculate and assess on a fundamentally different post-transaction private-market valuation framework when the Company is no longer publicly listed".

The firm emphasised that their approach is consistent with industry practices and based on solid professional judgment.

data.title: President Shanmugaratnam urges ‘new green revolution’ to secure food future
data.description:
Singapore must reduce water usage and cut methane and greenhouse gas emissions.

President Tharman Shanmugaratnam said that Singapore needs to revolutionise food systems to sustain food supply amidst growing demand.

Speaking at the Philanthropy Asia Summit on 5 May in a dialogue with Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates, Shanmugaratnam said Singapore needs a “new green revolution” that delivers higher yields while using fewer resources and producing fewer emissions.

He said the new approach must “uses less water, allows more moisture to stay in the soil, involves less methane emissions from rice cultivation especially, and less greenhouse gas emissions in general [but] still gives farmers a better yield”.

“We have reached the limits of traditional ways of producing food, both by ordinary smallholder farmers as well as in industrial agriculture. We've reached the limits because it is too large a source of greenhouse gas emissions; we can't keep encroaching into natural forests and other natural ecosystems; and also because soil health has deteriorated very significantly,” he said.

Temasek Trust’s Philanthropy Asia Alliance has launched the Decarbonising Rice initiative, which uses new seed varieties or new strains that are more resistant to droughts and floods.

This cultivation technique results in a 50% reduction in water requirements and 20% to 50% reductions in methane emissions.

“We have about 1.7 billion people being added to the world’s population in the next 25 years. In fact, in the next 10 years, we have about 800 million people. And we have a much larger increase in food and nutrition demand – by some 50-75% over the next 25 years,” the president said.

On the same day, the Gates Foundation, the non-profit organisation founded by Bill Gates, announced it would open a new office in Singapore to expand its regional presence.

During the discussion, Gates noted that Singapore is home to many committed philanthropists and strong family values.

“And so having something even beyond whatever the family business is—having the family philanthropy, getting the family members working together and being proud about what they're able to achieve—I think we're seeing a big increase in that,” he said.
